title: Intense Smoked Dutch Turkey Stew
description: A dense, fermented stew with smoky turkey and beet broth, perfect for a romantic Dutch meal that stays FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat until hot but not smoking.
Season the turkey breast pieces with smoked paprika, smoked sea salt, black pepper, and thyme.
Sear the turkey in the hot oil until golden on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Add the sliced carrots and celery to the pot and cook, stirring, until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the fermented beet puree and low-FODMAP vegetable broth, stirring to combine and deglaze the pan.
Return the turkey to the pot, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, reduce heat to low, and cover.
Cook, uncovered, for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the turkey is tender and the flavors meld.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional smoked sea salt or pepper if needed before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
